stories.title: Knicks 108, 76ers 102 — Brunson, Anunoby Lead New York to 2-0 Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
No Embiid. Still 25 lead changes. Still 14 ties. The 76ers pushed the Knicks all night.

Then Brunson hit clutch buckets in a 9-0 run.

Knicks 108, 76ers 102. New York leads 2-0. #Knicks #76ers #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Brunson 26. Anunoby 24. KAT 20/10/7. Maxey 26 on the other end.

The 76ers gave everything without Embiid — but New York had just enough.

Knicks lead the series 2-0. #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Philadelphia 76ers played without Joel Embiid last night — and they nearly pulled off the upset anyway.

Twenty-five lead changes. Fourteen ties. A back-and-forth thriller that looked nothing like the 39-point blowout in Game 1.

But the Knicks are built for moments like these. Jalen Brunson (26 pts) hit clutch buckets when the game was on the line. OG Anunoby (24 pts) was relentless on both ends. And Karl-Anthony Towns put up a near-triple-double (20/10/7) to anchor the effort.

The 76ers' Tyrese Maxey (26 pts) gave everything he had. Without Embiid — out with a right ankle sprain and hip soreness — Philadelphia showed real fight. But a 9-0 Knicks run late sealed it: 108-102.

New York leads the series 2-0 heading to Philadelphia for Game 3. The 76ers NEED Embiid back. The Knicks NEED to close this out before Philly figures anything out.

What was your biggest takeaway from Game 2?

article.headline: Brunson, Anunoby Lead Knicks Past 76ers 108-102 to Take Commanding 2-0 Series Lead

stories.title: Spurs 133, Timberwolves 95 — Wembanyama Leads Historic Blowout to Tie Series

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
38 points. That's how badly the Spurs beat the Timberwolves last night.

Wembanyama: 19 pts, 15 reb. Castle: 21. Fox: 16. San Antonio shot 50% from the floor.

Worst playoff loss in Wolves franchise history. Series tied 1-1. #Spurs #Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Timberwolves had a great story going. A dramatic Game 1 win. Anthony Edwards returning early from injury and delivering in the fourth quarter.

Then the Spurs happened.

San Antonio torched Minnesota 133-95 on Wednesday night — the worst playoff loss in Timberwolves franchise history. Victor Wembanyama was everywhere: 19 points, 15 rebounds, 2 blocks. Stephon Castle added 21. De'Aaron Fox chipped in 16. The Spurs shot 50% from the floor and 41% from three.

Minnesota had four players score 12 points. That's it.

San Antonio's 133-point performance was their highest-scoring playoff game since they beat Denver 145-105 back on May 4, 1983. A 43-year gap between Spurs postseason fireworks.

Series tied 1-1. Game 3 moves to San Antonio. The Wolves need to answer — and fast.

Can Anthony Edwards and Minnesota flip the script at the AT&T Center?

article.headline: Wembanyama Leads Historic Blowout: Spurs Crush Timberwolves 133-95 to Even Series

stories.title: Pistons vs. Cavaliers Game 2 Tonight — Detroit Chases 2-0 Lead at Home

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Pistons won Game 1 by 10. Now they're home with a chance to go up 2-0.

Cavs need Mitchell and Harden to flip the script tonight.

Game 2. 7 PM ET. Prime Video. #Pistons #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Detroit is one win away from a commanding 2-0 series lead — and tonight they're playing at home with the crowd behind them.

In Game 1, Cade Cunningham (23 pts, 7 ast) and Tobias Harris (20 pts, 8 reb) powered the Pistons to a 111-101 victory. The Pistons went to the free-throw line 35 times. They forced 19 Cleveland turnovers. It was an old-school, physical Detroit performance.

The Cavaliers weren't done, though. Donovan Mitchell (23 pts) and James Harden — who dropped 13 of his 22 points in the fourth quarter alone — made it interesting. But Detroit held on.

Now the question is simple: Can the Cavs make the adjustments? Kevin Huerter (hip) is questionable tonight, and Cleveland will need his shooting to open up the floor.

Game 2 tips off tonight at 7:00 PM ET on Prime Video. Detroit. The crowd. The moment.

Who takes Game 2 — Pistons or Cavs?

article.headline: Pistons vs. Cavaliers Game 2 Preview: Detroit Chases 2-0 Series Lead at Home Tonight

stories.title: Thunder vs. Lakers Game 2 Tonight — OKC Looks to Double Lead Without Luka

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
No Luka. No Jalen Williams. Thunder favored by 15.5.

LeBron dropped 27 in Game 1 — OKC still won by 18.

Game 2 tonight. 9:30 PM ET. Amazon Prime. #Lakers #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
LeBron James scored 27 points on 12-of-17 shooting in Game 1. And the Lakers still lost by 18.

That's the challenge facing Los Angeles tonight against the Oklahoma City Thunder.

Chet Holmgren went off for 24 points, 12 rebounds, and 3 blocks. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander added 18 points and 6 assists. The Thunder were clinical, dominant, and efficient — without Jalen Williams, who is still recovering from a hamstring injury of his own.

And the Lakers? They're playing without Luka Doncic, who revealed this week that doctors gave him an eight-week recovery timeline from his April 2 hamstring strain. Eight weeks from April 2 is the end of May — deep into the playoffs, well past this series.

Thunder are 15.5-point favorites in Game 2. Game tips off at 9:30 PM ET on Amazon Prime.

The Lakers' best chance tonight: LeBron going nuclear again. Is that enough?

article.headline: Thunder vs. Lakers Game 2 Preview: OKC Looks to Put Series Away as Luka Watch Intensifies

stories.title: Luka Reveals 8-Week Timeline — Lakers Star Unlikely to Return This Series

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Luka Doncic says doctors gave him an 8-week timeline when he got hurt April 2.

Eight weeks from April 2 is late May. He hasn't cleared contact drills yet.

The Lakers may not see No. 77 again this postseason. #Lakers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Luka Doncic has finally broken his silence on his injury — and what he said is not what Lakers fans wanted to hear.

Doncic revealed this week that when he suffered his Grade 2 left hamstring strain on April 2, doctors told him to expect an eight-week recovery timeline.

Do the math: eight weeks from April 2 puts his return in the last week of May 2026.

The current Thunder-Lakers series began on May 5. It could be over well before Luka's timeline is up — even in a best-case scenario for LA. And that's assuming he heals perfectly, which is far from guaranteed. Doncic confirmed he has not yet been cleared for contact drills, the final threshold before a player gets the green light to return.

In an effort to speed the process, Luka flew to Spain to receive specialized cell therapy from Dr. Javier Barrio — the sports doctor at Real Madrid, Luka's former club. The desperation behind that trip says everything.

LeBron James is giving everything he has. The team is trying. But this postseason is running out of road for the King's Lakers.

Do you think Luka comes back at all in the 2026 playoffs?

article.headline: Luka Reveals 8-Week Recovery Timeline — Lakers Star Unlikely to Return in Thunder Series
